author: vrtmrz
description: Utility to put any files in and out under your vault.

manifest.name: Screwdriver

name: Screwdriver

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.0.13

14th July, 2026

### Reliability

- Reuse successful parent-directory checks when restoring sibling files.
- Stop an affected write when parent-directory preparation fails, rather than continuing with later storage operations.
- Preserve the existing skip, timestamp, text, and binary behaviour for successful restores.

### Digging the weeds

- Isolated Vault discovery, reads, restores, and frontmatter policy behind focused capabilities.
- Routed frontmatter updates through the shared Fancy Kit Vault capability.
- Added App-free coverage for filtering, ignored subtrees, binary data, operation ordering, failure propagation, and frontmatter updates.
- Extended the local real-Obsidian restore scenario for nested siblings, binary data, newer-file preservation, and traversal rejection.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.0.12

10th July, 2026

### Changed

- Refined the add-folder flow with a shared target picker and Markdown confirmation dialogue.
- Made dismissal of the plug-in data confirmation follow the existing safe default.

### Safety

- Reject unsafe absolute, traversal, and platform-invalid restore paths before accessing the Vault.
- Continue restoring later safe files after an unsafe restore block is skipped.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.0.12-fancy.1

10th July, 2026

I am carrying out a bit of refactoring to improve robustness and maintainability. This release is intended for a comprehensive review—including CI and testing with a real Obsidian instance—and does not offer any particular benefits. You are welcome to skip it.

I, vrtmrz, will review this release. Thank you as always!

### Changed

- Refactored target selection and plug-in data confirmation to use Fancy Kit UI interactions.
- Added restore-path validation so unsafe paths are skipped before Vault access while later safe blocks continue to restore.
- Preserved portable `.obsidian/...` paths by mapping them below the active `configDir`.

### Reduced actual-device verification

Shared picker and Markdown dialogue behaviour has already passed desktop/mobile checks through DiffZip and TagFolder. ScrewDriver's harnesses and local real-Obsidian scenarios cover Include, Exclude, dismissal, path variants, `configDir` mapping, and safe continuation. Only these consumer-specific smoke paths remain:

- [ ] Install `0.0.12-fancy.1` through BRAT, enable ScrewDriver, restart Obsidian, and confirm existing notes, settings, targets, and filters remain intact
- [ ] On desktop, add one plug-in folder, choose **Include**, and confirm the selected target plus `main\.js$`, `manifest\.json$`, `styles\.css$`, and `data\.json$` filters are present
- [ ] On desktop, restore the fixture below and confirm an unsafe-path Notice appears, no file is created outside the Vault, and `Screwdriver-preview/safe.txt` is restored with exactly `safe content`
- [ ] On mobile, enable and restart ScrewDriver, then confirm the target picker and Markdown confirmation dialogue open without errors
- [ ] Record the Obsidian version, BRAT version, OS/device, and test date

A separate Exclude/dismissal matrix, ordinary restore, and non-default `configDir` run are not required. If a non-default configuration directory is readily available, its `.obsidian/...` mapping may be checked additionally.

Paste this fixture into a new Markdown note:

````markdown
---
targets: []
urls: []
ignores: []
filters: []
adjustObsidianDir: true
skipNewFile: false
skipOldFile: false
---

```screwdriver:../screwdriver-preview-escape.txt:plain:0
unsafe content
```

```screwdriver:Screwdriver-preview/safe.txt:plain:0
safe content
```
````

I will record UI results on [PR #6](https://github.com/vrtmrz/obsidian-screwdriver/pull/6) and restore-path results on [PR #7](https://github.com/vrtmrz/obsidian-screwdriver/pull/7).
releases.name: 0.0.12-fancy.1

## 0.0.10

Minor updates and ensuring compliance with community reviews. Although some errors are now handled correctly, there are no significant changes in behaviour.

- Adjusting `.obsidian` folder during export/restore
- Skipping old files / new files

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.0.8 
 Improved:
  - Vague messages have got a bit better.
  - Create dump template is now separated
    - For the local file, and for the remote file.
  - When adding the target, filters are automatically determined by its type.
